So that everyone understands the situation about the cat trying to climb onto the roof: first of all, this is NOT animal abuse, since animal abuse must be caused intentionally. These are simply the cat’s own mistakes, and it falls because the distance is not enough.  Second, the man in the original video is just recording and cannot help the cat because the roof is too far away. Even if he had picked it up and thrown it toward the roof, the cat would have fallen anyway.  And third, the person who reposted the original video in this compilation knows that it is sad, since he even adds an emoji, which is this “😭”. And yes, it may be a bit cruel to include it in a funny cats compilation, but even he knows it is something sad. There is no need to report it, because the rooftop cat video does not belong to him and is only included in his video.

Normally otters have rocks they use and play with and use for buoyancy for a little bit (they also smash shellfish with them). There was an otter at Birmingham (UK, not under Sharia law, actually being gentrified) Sealife centre who had kept the same rock for years. They love to play with them. I visited the place a few times and the same little dude was always chilling, always doing curls with his rock, floating on his back.  I suspect he was either working on his escape or was the most ripped otter in the yard. Definitely was his enclosure. I think they called him Oscar or something.

My calico would weave between the bars of our stairwell like it was a game, but would always wait for us to look at her first before doing it specifically to get a reaction out of us. If I went downstairs and she was upstairs, she would hook her hind legs around the bars and hang upside down like a bat to reach my head as I was walking up, typically to get a reaction out of us. So we weaved netting around every bar in the banister to keep her from being able to do that. She figured out how to shimmy the net up to slid underneath it (even though it was tacked down into the wooden frame) and sit on the inside of the net making it impossible for us to grab her while she straddled the railing. She just enjoyed the comedy of getting a reaction out of us.

You have just k!lled that turtle😢  Never ever pick a freshly hatched turtle to drop in the water/see.  They need to crawl themselves for multiple reasons. 1. This crawl helps them imprint on the beach’s magnetic and environmental cues 2.It also helps them develop strength and coordination needed to survive 4.Imprinting really matters The person who told you this was mostly correct: Sea turtles use Earth’s magnetic field and other cues to later return to the same region to lay eggs as adults Interfering with their natural journey can disrupt this process 5.Higher risk of death Handling hatchlings: Causes stress Can damage their fragile bodies Exposes them to bacteria or sunscreen/chemicals on human hands This reduces their already very low survival rate (only ~1 in 1,000 reaches adulthood).
